title: malaysian gobo bao
description: a malaysian-inspired dish featuring gobo, served in a warm, yeasty bun.
instructions: in a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, yeast, and salt.
add the water and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
preheat a kazan or a large saucepan with a lid over medium heat.
add the coconut oil, onion, ginger, and garlic to the kazan and sauté until the onion is translucent.
add the gobo to the kazan and stir-fry for 5 minutes.
add the soy sauce and sesame oil to the kazan and stir-fry for 1 minute.
punch down the dough and divide it into 4-6 equal pieces.
shape each piece into a ball and then flatten it slightly into a disk shape.
place a disk of dough in the kazan and add a spoonful of the gobo filling to the center of the dough.
fold the dough over the filling to form a bun and press the edges together to seal the bun.
cook the buns for 5-7 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown and cooked through.
